column1: # GrainMasterV4.tsv -- per-grain dispatch and semantic table for EmulatR V4
# Project: EmulatR -- Alpha AXP / EV6 Architecture Emulator (V4)
# Copyright (C) 2025, 2026 eNVy Systems, Inc.  All rights reserved.
# Licensed under eNVy Systems Non-Commercial License v1.1
# Commercial use prohibited without separate license.
# Single source of truth for the GrainFactory codegen.  Each row describes
# one Alpha AXP operation: the encoding fields that identify it, the
# dispatch arm it lives in, the functional box that owns its leaf, and
# the semantic flags it carries on the InstructionGrain.
# Supersedes V3's GrainMaster.tsv (kept as historical reference; do not
# extend).  The V3 schema lacked a flags column, expressed personality
# via the "_64" mnemonic suffix, and contained duplicates and
# misclassifications that V4 codegen would refuse.
# Tab-separated.  Lines starting with '#' are comments.  Blank lines
#   mnemonic        Alpha mnemonic; case-sensitive.  May repeat across
#                   rows when the same encoding exists under both PAL
#   subDecode       Hex; sub-decode field value.  0x0 when dispatchKind
#                   is Direct (no sub-decode).  Field width depends on
#   dispatchKind    Direct | IntArith | IntLogical | IntShift | IntMul |
#                   FpTiExt | Pal | HwMfpr | HwLd | HwMtpr | HwRei |
#   format          Mem | Bra | Op | Fp | Pal | Hw | Jmp | Misc
#                   Must agree with the S_xxxFormat flag in the flags
#   box             Ibox | Ebox | Fbox | Mbox | Cbox | PalBox
#                   Functional box that owns the leaf.  Lowered by
#                   codegen to a C++ namespace (eBox, fBox, mBox, cBox,
#                   iBox, palBox) and a directory under {box}Lib/grains/.
#   flags           Pipe-separated list of S_xxx flag names defined in
#                   reference undefined flags or violate mutex groups.
# Some CALL_PAL function codes have different semantics under Tru64 vs
# OpenVMS PAL personalities.  Two ways to encode this:
#   - One row with both S_PalTru64 and S_PalVms set: encoding behaves
#   - Two rows with the same (primaryOpcode, subDecode) pair, one
#     tagged S_PalTru64 and one tagged S_PalVms: encoding has distinct
#     leaf semantics per personality.  Codegen synthesizes leaf names
#     g_palTableVms) accordingly, and the CPU selects one at boot.
#   - Personality-agnostic instructions (the common case) set neither
#     flag.  Outside CALL_PAL, no row should set either flag.

column1: # inexact).  All variants of a given base op share a single leaf via
# the leafBase column -- 16 GrainMaster rows route to one execAdds (etc.)
# leaf, which inspects the modifier bits at runtime to apply the right
# The block below was emitted by codegen/expandFpVariants.py; re-run
# that script to regenerate, extend the BASES table to add new ops,
# extend the MODIFIERS table to add variants.
# --- IEEE compares (base form, T-format double precision; result is 2.0 or 0.0) ---
# --- IEEE conversions (base form; UNARY, source Fb) ---
# --- IEEE conversion qualifier variants (UNARY, source Fb) ---
# --- IEEE square root (base form; UNARY, source Fb; opcode 0x14) ---
# --- ITOFF (VAX int->F move; eBox) + SQRT qualifier variants (UNARY, source Fb) ---
# VAX floating point -- opcode 0x15 (FLTV).  Complete architectural set, every
# opcode.func variant as a distinct row (no consolidation).  Source: Alpha ARM
# Appendix C numerical grid.  arith/compare = binary (Fa,Fb); CVT* = unary (Fb).
# OpenVMS default double = G_float, default single = F_float -- the OS gate.
# Leaves are a later phase (no leafBase -> each stubs independently).
# FP logical and FPCR access -- opcode 0x17 (FLTL)

column1: # --- FP conditional branches (opcode-only; test Fa interpreted as a float) ---
# CALL_PAL -- complete function-code map per AARM Table C-15
# Source: alpha_arch_ref.txt (Alpha Architecture Reference Manual), Table
# C-15 "PALcode Opcodes in Numerical Order", lines ~47644-47873.  AARM's
# Opcode16 is primary.function-code in hex; Opcode10 is the same value in
# decimal.  "--" in a personality column means the function does not exist
# Rule: ONE row per unique opcode.  Personality flags qualify which
# personalities exhibit the opcode; the description column records per-
# personality spelling and any semantic divergence.  All three flags
# (S_PalTru64, S_PalVms, S_PalLinux) are independently set per AARM
#   - S_PalTru64 only: AARM Tru64-UNIX column has a mnemonic, Linux is "--"
#   - S_PalLinux only: AARM Linux column has a mnemonic, Tru64 is "--" (rare)
#   - S_PalTru64 | S_PalLinux: same mnemonic and semantic in both columns
#   - S_PalVms: VMS-only function (most of the MFPR_/MTPR_ family)
#   - All three: AARM has all three columns populated (HALT, BPT, etc.)
# Linux="--" exceptions in current TSV: MTPR_DATFX (0x2E), REI (0x92).
# Dispatch posture: default = S_PalEntry|S_ChangesPC|S_Uncond, divert via
# execCallPalDispatch to PALcode at palBase + entry vector.  Custom
# intrinsic leaves are pinned only where V4 v1 needs the function before
# PALcode is loaded (HALT, LDQP, STQP, CSERVE, MFPR_VPTB, MTPR_VPTB,
# WTINT, BPT, CHME, CHMK) -- those rows live earlier in this file.
# Semantic-divergence rows (same opcode, different operations per
# personality) carry both personality flags; the runtime/leaf MUST
# dispatch on personality.  V4 v1 currently honors the VMS semantic only
# on these rows (the legacy execMfprWhami intrinsic stays VMS-side); the
# Tru64/Linux alternates land in "next steps" once the runtime grows

column1: # End of first wave (~50 rows; exercises every dispatchKind and every box).
# Subsequent waves to add: full INTA/INTL/INTS/INTM coverage (V3 had 80+
# rows in opcodes 0x10-0x13), full FLTI coverage (V3 had ~150 FP rows
# spanning all rounding-mode and trap-mode variants), full PAL function
# code map for both personalities, and the remaining BWX/CIX/MVI/FIX
